Title: Deregulation of phenylalanine biosynthesis evolved with the emergence of vascular plants

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts arogenate to phenylalanine (ADT activity) and also converts prephenate to phenylpyruvate (PDT activity).
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylalanine biosynthesis via the arogenate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step through relaxed feedback inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Proteinogenic amino acid and precursor for phenylpropanoids/lignin-related specialized metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly increases phenylalanine accumulation; enzyme is weakly inhibited by phenylalanine compared with type-I ADTs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium infiltration; samples collected 72 h after infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid-targeted enzyme (transit peptide reported and removed for recombinant expression)
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ADT/PDT family enzyme containing regulatory ACT domain with type-II/PAC-variant phenylalanine-binding region
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assays for ADT and PDT activities; IC50 determination for phenylalanine inhibition; transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves with UPLC/MS phenylalanine profiling; western blot confirmation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising metabolic-engineering target to elevate phenylalanine supply and downstream phenylpropanoid/lignin-derived metabolites by deploying deregulated ADT alleles or editing the ACT/PAC regulatory region
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpADT-C is a type-II ADT with strongly relaxed feedback inhibition by phenylalanine. 2. Its ADT activity showed no substantial inhibition up to 100 μM phenylalanine, and PDT IC50 was about 320 μM. 3. Overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves caused about 145-fold phenylalanine accumulation relative to GFP control, far exceeding type-I enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Carries sequence changes in the ACT/PAC regulatory region associated with deregulation; used as the main model for type-II enzymes in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts arogenate to phenylalanine and prephenate to phenylpyruvate.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylalanine biosynthesis via the arogenate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key regulated terminal step under strong feedback inhibition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Proteinogenic amino acid and precursor for phenylpropanoids/lignin-related specialized metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Moderately increases phenylalanine accumulation but is strongly feedback-inhibited by phenylalanine
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium infiltration; samples collected 72 h after infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid-targeted enzyme (transit peptide reported and removed for recombinant expression)
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ADT/PDT family enzyme with ACT regulatory domain and type-I PAC sequence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assays, kinetic analysis of phenylalanine inhibition, domain swapping, transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana and metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful reference regulated allele; comparison with deregulated variants identifies residues for gene editing to increase phenylalanine flux without replacing the enzyme core
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpADT-G is a type-I ADT strongly inhibited by phenylalanine. 2. ADT IC50 was 27.6 μM and PDT IC50 was 47.7 μM, indicating tight feedback control. 3. Overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana increased phenylalanine only about 4-fold relative to control, much less than type-II enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Serves as the canonical tightly regulated isoform; inhibition was described as apparently uncompetitive for PDT activity.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts arogenate to phenylalanine; also classified here as a type-II ADT used for in planta phenylalanine accumulation tests.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supports key control via deregulated feedback behavior
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Proteinogenic amino acid and precursor for phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Very strongly increases phenylalanine accumulation when overexpressed in planta
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium infiltration; samples collected 72 h after infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana and UPLC/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate enzyme for boosting phenylalanine pool in engineering strategies
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PpADT-A behaved as a type-II ADT in planta. 2. Overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves increased phenylalanine about 160-fold versus control. 3. This supports the high metabolic impact of deregulated ADT isoforms.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not kinetically characterized in detail in Results, but used as a representative deregulated type-II enzyme in planta.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Regulated type-I enzyme; not established as sole rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Proteinogenic amino acid and phenylpropanoid precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression modestly increased phenylalanine accumulation; recombinant enzyme was strongly inhibited by phenylalanine as a type-I ADT
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium infiltration; 72 h after infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ADT family enzyme with type-I ACT domain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay at 100 μM phenylalanine; transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana and metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Baseline regulated allele for comparison with deregulated ADTs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtADT1 grouped with type-I ADTs and its recombinant activity dropped strongly in the presence of 100 μM phenylalanine. 2. Overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana increased phenylalanine about 32-fold versus control. 3. It had much smaller impact than type-II enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as representative flowering plant type-I ADT.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Proteinogenic amino acid and phenylpropanoid precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression caused only a small increase in phenylalanine compared with deregulated type-II enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium infiltration; 72 h after infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana and metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Less effective than deregulated ADTs for increasing phenylalanine pool
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtADT2 overexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana increased phenylalanine about 6-fold relative to control. 2. Its effect was far weaker than that of type-II enzymes such as PpADT-C and PpADT-A.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included only in in planta overexpression comparison.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Proteinogenic amino acid and phenylpropanoid precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Recombinant enzyme showed little inhibition at 100 μM phenylalanine, supporting its classification as type-II/deregulated ADT
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assay with 100 μM phenylalanine
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ADT family enzyme with type-II ACT domain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay; co-expression analysis in ATTED-II mentioned
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Co-expression analysis in ATTED-II v11.0 based on 12,686 ATH1 arrays and 14,741 RNA-seq runs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential endogenous Arabidopsis target for engineering increased phenylalanine flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtADT4 clustered with type-II ADTs. 2. Its recombinant ADT activity was not substantially affected by 100 μM phenylalanine, consistent with deregulated behavior.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as representative flowering plant type-II ADT.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Chimeric PpADT-G carrying the PAC domain from PpADT-C; catalyzes ADT and PDT reactions.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Demonstrates control by the PAC domain
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Proteinogenic amino acid and phenylpropanoid precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acquired relaxed phenylalanine inhibition after PAC-domain swap
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro phenylalanine inhibition assays; transient Agrobacterium infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ADT/PDT family enzyme with swapped ACT/PAC regulatory region
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Domain swapping, recombinant inhibition kinetics, transient overexpression/metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Proof-of-concept that editing the PAC region can convert tightly regulated ADTs into deregulated forms
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Swapping the PAC domain from PpADT-C into PpADT-G strongly relaxed feedback inhibition. 2. ADT activity showed no substantial inhibition up to 100 μM phenylalanine and PDT IC50 approached 390 μM. 3. This demonstrates that the PAC region largely determines deregulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chimeric experimental construct rather than native gene.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Chimeric PpADT-C carrying the PAC domain from PpADT-G; catalyzes ADT and PDT reactions.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Demonstrates control by the PAC domain
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Proteinogenic amino acid and phenylpropanoid precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Lost deregulation and became more sensitive to phenylalanine inhibition after receiving the type-I PAC domain
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro phenylalanine inhibition assays; transient Agrobacterium infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ADT/PDT family enzyme with swapped ACT/PAC regulatory region
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Domain swapping, recombinant inhibition kinetics, transient overexpression/metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Shows that introducing type-I PAC residues can restore strong feedback control
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Swapping the type-I PAC domain into PpADT-C increased phenylalanine sensitivity. 2. ADT IC50 became about 52.8 μM and PDT IC50 about 36.1 μM. 3. In planta phenylalanine accumulation dropped to a level comparable to wild-type PpADT-G.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chimeric experimental construct rather than native gene.
